purpose: Smallest canonical memory ledger that preserves load-bearing state across audits and future sealed builds without recreating context from chat history.
universality_rule: This file may be copied into future audited builds. It must store only durable, load-bearing audit memory. Anything reconstructable from other root files should be omitted unless repeated reconstruction would create drift risk.

anti_bloat_rule.scope: this build and every future build the engine seals or audits
rules: Every zip should contain no more than 10 root files maximum if possible.
Always update and merge what can be merged into a single file without breaking load-bearing structure.
Do not add a new root file unless it carries unique governing force that cannot be safely merged into an existing root file.
Prefer strengthening an existing root file over spawning a sibling file.
Any proposed expansion must justify why merge is impossible or unsafe.

governing_rules: No artifact receives governing force, supersession force, or seal consideration until it has a ledger row and survives contradiction resolution.
Live Canon Candidate means eligible for further audit and possible promotion, not currently governing unless separately granted governing force.
Keep files as minimal as possible to prevent bloating. Anything that can exist in a single file should do so.
No seal until the seal event itself is auditable.
Do not rebuild the archive unless a new load-bearing root is instantiated or a load-bearing root materially changes.
next_legal_triggers: populate next receipt object (R-BATTERY, R-MUTATION, R-REPLAY, or R-DRIFT)
materially change authority or claim graph

must_not_store: duplicate file contents available elsewhere in root

recent_updates.change: R-REPLAY-001 upgraded with hash-backed input/spec comparison via EVT-009

last_change: Resolved receipt-history governance wrinkle by separating append-only history from current canonical receipt state in RECEIPTS.json
